publications.title: The Lucky Penny
publications.description:
A self published post-apocalyptic novel.
Penny never thought she’d have family. Penny lost family when Penny little. Penny scared when taken away from home. Penny stay quiet. Penny lost, Penny found. Now Penny have new family. Penny brave. Penny tell her story.
Julietta Milard’s life had been rather mundane up until a few years ago when she found herself in the small town of Waterwealt. Though she had intended to move on to bigger and better things, a beautiful museum preserved in time had her sprouting roots rather than traversing the Wastes further.
Having helped the sleepy town with its various mechanical-based problems, Julietta thought found herself in a smooth routine. That was until the universe decided to throw a curveball her way when about two cycles later a small girl shows up at her doorstep, sick, injured, and unable to speak with only a penny necklace as any identification.
Nearly a cycle and a half later, the young girl, whom Julietta named Penny, has recovered but remains mute despite her best efforts. On top of taking care of little Penny, restoring the museum, and trying to find a solution for the now increasingly weakening water pressure in town, Julietta is faced with another issue. A stranger has come to town, a ‘doctor’ named Charles Hawthorne, who seems to think the Arcane is real. Brushing him off, she finds herself questioning what the world is coming to. That is until the world as she knows it to come crashing down around her and vanishes in a cloud of dust.

projects.title: Proof of Concept Machine Learning Data Retrieval System
projects.description:
Designed and programmed a machine learning based data retrieval system using SWI-Prolog as a proof of concept based on a fictional program called D3B.dsk from a YouTube mystery series called Project 863.
As an experiment, I attempted to recreate a more simplistic version of D3b.dsk using a 1987 open-source machine learning-style programming language known as Swi-Prolog. Using this language, I have developed a program called ST3PH.dsk.
Originally, I spent a couple of weeks researching and programming to create standalone runnable programs for Windows and Linux. I eventually rewrote the entire thing in Python and deployed it on Heroku.Its base functionality allowed the user to navigate "directories" and enter an event name, year, month & year, full date, or up to two key terms for cross-referencing to retrieve events on the timeline created for the show in those directories. It has three knowledge bases that can be individually or all searched at once. The CSVs (standalone) and Database (web app) for these were  updated as more information from the show is released.
It was decommissioned when the series ended, but its code remains available on GitHub (Web App) and MediaFire (Standalone)

projects.title: Minecraft: Java Edition Custom Maps (Video Games)
projects.description:
I am a self taught Minecraft Map maker. I use Minecraft as a game engine to create small video games.
SolveIT Series - Singleplayer Adventure
This is a detective style series of maps. The player take on the role of 'Rookie' as they solve case after case, all ending with notes from a friend raising concerns about SolveIT Inc. Each case has a custom story and custom puzzles to solve.
S.I. Files Series - Singleplayer Adventure
This is the sequel to SolveIT. The player solves 'cases' that are spread out into 2 or 3 maps. This series currently has 5 maps.
The Tester - Singleplayer Adventure
It is the year 3000. A lot has changed in the world, particularly in the realm of Virtual Reality.
You are invited by one of the original testers of the VR system to learn about it. Simply referred to as
The Tester, you join them as they recount how they tested the system...and the various things that ensued. 
Mastering the Mind- Singleplayer Puzzle
Solve 6 different types of puzzles based on Emotions, Memory, Imagination, Perspective, Coordination, and Logic in order to reach the control center of your brain and Master the Mind!
Chaos's Box - Singleplayer or 2 Player Adventure
After years of research and searching you have found it, the infamous Chaos's box.
You and your co-worker are more than eager to open the box, yet your other co-worker George is more wary...the story can't be true.
What could possibly go wrong opening a box?
SecureNET: The Grey Hat Origins - Singleplayer Adventure

projects.title: SecureNET the Grey Hat Origins - Cyber Security Educational Game in Minecraft
projects.description: For my cyber security course I created a cyber-security educational game in Minecraft for my semester long project. I applied the concept of gamification in order to teach the basics of cyber security. In addition to the game I create an anonymous online survey that players could fill out to help gauge player learning. I then ran analysis on the survey data and determined the gamification to be a success.
